title: AI Outcomes Manager
must_haves: 5+ years in roles blending business and technology (product, analytics, data, engineering, solutions)
Craft effective prompts and guide AI agents for real workflows; understand LLM capabilities/limits
Enough technical depth to work with engineers (not on critical path for production code)
Consultative, customer-facing: executive sponsors, discovery workshops, AI roadmaps
hard_blockers: 5+ yrs explicitly in product/analytics/data/engineering/solutions — Ryan's years are GTM/community/marketing, not these tech-adjacent functions
Enterprise SaaS adoption/consulting role (100+ enterprise connectors) — no enterprise solutions/delivery background
Executive-sponsor enterprise consulting cadence vs Ryan's startup community-led GTM
met_strengths: Strong prompting / AI-agent building fluency (Claude Code) — directly matches the prompt/agent must-have and AI exercise
Product sense + user empathy from growth/lifecycle work
~6.4 yrs total clears nominal year count
one_line_verdict: Agentic/prompting strength is a real edge, but the 5+ yrs must sit in product/data/eng/solutions and it's an enterprise-SaaS adoption consulting role — Ryan's tech-adjacent years don't match the gate.
fetch_note: Confirmed open via Greenhouse (gleanwork 4613925005), London hybrid 1 day/wk, 5+ yrs business+tech. Glean = enterprise Work AI platform. AI-focused exercise in interview process.
